approvalTitle: M3 Tasks Document - Granular Task Breakdown with LLM Prompts
content:
# Tasks Document

- [ ] 1. Create mcp-client-quest project structure
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\mcp-client-quest\package.json
  - Initialize project with separate client (React + Vite) and server (Express) directories
  - Set up TypeScript 5.9.3, React 19.2.0, Vite 7.2.4, Express dependencies
  - Purpose: Establish project foundation for dashboard migration
  - _Leverage: design.md (Component 1 architecture)_
  - _Requirements: 1.1_
  - _Prompt: Role: DevOps Engineer with expertise in Node.js project setup | Task: Create mcp-client-quest project structure following design.md Component 1 architecture, initializing separate package.json for client and server with appropriate dependencies | Restrictions: Must follow structure.md naming conventions, use specified versions, configure for both local and remote deployment | Success: Directory structure matches design.md, package.json files have correct dependencies, project compiles without errors_

- [ ] 2. Extract React frontend from mcp-server-quest
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\mcp-client-quest\client\src\
  - Copy React components from mcp-server-quest/src/dashboard/client/ to mcp-client-quest/client/
  - Update all import paths to work in new structure
  - Purpose: Move dashboard frontend to dedicated MCP client
  - _Leverage: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\mcp-server-quest\src\dashboard\client\_
  - _Requirements: 1.1_
  - _Prompt: Role: Frontend Developer with expertise in React migration | Task: Extract React frontend from mcp-server-quest/src/dashboard/client/ to mcp-client-quest/client/, updating all import paths and ensuring components work in new structure | Restrictions: Must preserve all existing functionality, do not modify component logic, maintain file organization | Success: All React components extracted successfully, imports resolved correctly, frontend compiles without errors_

- [ ] 3. Create Express backend structure
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\mcp-client-quest\server\src\index.ts
  - Create Express server with index.ts entry point, routes directory, and websocket.ts
  - Configure CORS and basic middleware for local and remote deployment
  - Purpose: Establish backend foundation for dashboard
  - _Leverage: design.md (Component 1 backend interfaces)_
  - _Requirements: 1.2_
  - _Prompt: Role: Backend Developer with expertise in Express.js | Task: Create Express backend structure in mcp-client-quest/server/ with index.ts entry point, routes directory, and websocket.ts, configuring CORS and basic middleware | Restrictions: Must use TypeScript, follow design.md patterns, configure for both local and remote deployment | Success: Express server starts successfully, CORS configured correctly, basic routing works_

- [ ] 4. Implement WebSocket server for real-time updates
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\mcp-client-quest\server\src\websocket.ts
  - Create WebSocket server using ws library with event handlers for quest.created, quest.updated, task.assigned, task.completed, approval.requested
  - Handle connection/disconnection gracefully and support multiple concurrent clients
  - Purpose: Enable real-time dashboard updates
  - _Leverage: design.md (Component 1 WebSocket interfaces)_
  - _Requirements: 1.3_
  - _Prompt: Role: Backend Developer with expertise in WebSocket and real-time communication | Task: Implement WebSocket server in websocket.ts using ws library, creating event handlers for quest.created, quest.updated, task.assigned, task.completed, approval.requested following design.md Component 1 interfaces | Restrictions: Must handle connection/disconnection gracefully, support multiple concurrent clients, ensure message delivery reliability | Success: WebSocket server accepts connections, events are broadcast correctly, clients receive real-time updates_

- [ ] 5. Replace direct API calls with KADI broker tool invocation
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\mcp-client-quest\client\src\services\QuestService.ts
  - Replace direct API calls in QuestService and TaskService with KADI broker tool invocation using MCP protocol
  - Use @modelcontextprotocol/sdk for tool invocation
  - Purpose: Enable dashboard to communicate via KADI broker
  - _Leverage: design.md (Component 1 interfaces), existing KADI integration patterns_
  - _Requirements: 1.2_
  - _Prompt: Role: Integration Engineer with expertise in MCP protocol and KADI broker | Task: Replace direct API calls in QuestService and TaskService with KADI broker tool invocation following design.md Component 1 interfaces, using MCP protocol for tool invocation | Restrictions: Must use @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, maintain existing service interface, handle connection errors gracefully | Success: All service methods use KADI broker, MCP protocol implemented correctly, tool invocation works end-to-end_

- [ ] 6. Implement WebSocket client for real-time updates
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\mcp-client-quest\client\src\services\WebSocketService.ts
  - Create WebSocket client with auto-reconnect logic and event subscription system
  - Implement exponential backoff for reconnection and support event unsubscription
  - Purpose: Enable dashboard to receive real-time updates
  - _Leverage: design.md (Component 1 WebSocket interfaces)_
  - _Requirements: 1.3_
  - _Prompt: Role: Frontend Developer with expertise in WebSocket client implementation | Task: Create WebSocket client in WebSocketService.ts with auto-reconnect logic and event subscription system following design.md Component 1 interfaces | Restrictions: Must handle disconnections gracefully, implement exponential backoff for reconnection, support event unsubscription | Success: WebSocket client connects successfully, auto-reconnect works, event subscription/unsubscription works correctly_

- [ ] 7. Test dashboard loads and displays data
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\mcp-client-quest\client\src\App.tsx
  - Test that dashboard loads successfully and displays quest/task data using mock data
  - Verify all React components render correctly in browser
  - Purpose: Validate frontend extraction success
  - _Leverage: Existing React components_
  - _Requirements: 1.1_
  - _Prompt: Role: QA Engineer with expertise in frontend testing | Task: Test that dashboard loads successfully and displays quest/task data using mock data, verifying all React components render correctly | Restrictions: Use mock data for testing, do not connect to backend yet, test in browser | Success: Dashboard loads without errors, all components render correctly, mock data displays properly_

- [ ] 8. Test approval workflow (approve/reject)
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\mcp-client-quest\client\src\components\ApprovalDialog.tsx
  - Test approval workflow end-to-end including approval request display, approve action, and reject action with reason
  - Verify KADI broker tool invocation works correctly
  - Purpose: Validate human-in-the-loop workflow
  - _Leverage: design.md (Component 1 interfaces)_
  - _Requirements: 1.6, 1.7_
  - _Prompt: Role: QA Engineer with expertise in workflow testing | Task: Test approval workflow end-to-end including approval request display, approve action, and reject action with reason, verifying KADI broker tool invocation works correctly | Restrictions: Must test both approve and reject paths, verify events are published correctly, test error handling | Success: Approval requests display correctly, approve/reject actions work, events are published to KADI broker_

- [ ] 9. Test error handling (connection failure)
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\mcp-client-quest\client\src\utils\errorHandler.ts
  - Test error handling for KADI broker offline and WebSocket disconnection scenarios
  - Verify dashboard displays appropriate error messages and retries automatically
  - Purpose: Validate error handling robustness
  - _Leverage: design.md (Error Handling section)_
  - _Requirements: 1.5_
  - _Prompt: Role: QA Engineer with expertise in error scenario testing | Task: Test error handling for KADI broker offline and WebSocket disconnection scenarios following design.md Error Handling section, verifying dashboard displays appropriate error messages and retries automatically | Restrictions: Must test both error scenarios, verify retry logic works, ensure user sees clear error messages | Success: Dashboard displays connection error when KADI broker is offline, auto-retry works, WebSocket reconnection works_

- [ ] 10. Extend AgentRole type and add role config loader in agents-library
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agents-library\src\types.ts, C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agents-library\src\role-config-loader.ts
  - Extend AgentRole type to support dynamic role names beyond the current hardcoded union type
  - Create RoleConfigLoader class that loads JSON role configuration files and validates them against schema
  - Add RoleConfig and ShadowRoleConfig TypeScript interfaces matching design.md data models
  - Purpose: Centralize role-related types and configuration loading in the shared library
  - _Leverage: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agents-library\src\types.ts (existing AgentRole type), design.md (Component 2 RoleConfig model)_
  - _Requirements: 2.1, 2.5_
  - _Prompt: Role: TypeScript Developer specializing in shared library design | Task: Extend AgentRole type in agents-library to support dynamic role names, create RoleConfigLoader class for loading and validating JSON role configs, and add RoleConfig/ShadowRoleConfig interfaces matching design.md data models | Restrictions: Must maintain backward compatibility with existing AgentRole consumers (agent-artist, shadow-agent-artist), do not break existing createWorkerAgent/createShadowAgent factories, follow existing agents-library code patterns | Success: AgentRole type supports dynamic roles, RoleConfigLoader loads and validates configs correctly, interfaces match design.md, existing consumers still work_

- [ ] 11. Migrate KADI event naming to generic pattern in agents-library
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agents-library\src\kadi-event-publisher.ts
  - Refactor kadi-event-publisher.ts to use generic event names (e.g., task.completed) instead of role-specific names (e.g., artist.task.completed)
  - Include agent metadata (agentId, agentRole) in event payload instead of event name
  - Update createWorkerAgent and createShadowAgent factories to use new event naming
  - Purpose: Support multiple agent-worker instances with same role without event name collisions
  - _Leverage: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agents-library\src\kadi-event-publisher.ts, C:\GitHub\AGENTS\kadi-core\ (event structure)_
  - _Requirements: 2.3, 2.4_
  - _Prompt: Role: Integration Engineer with expertise in event-driven architecture | Task: Refactor kadi-event-publisher.ts to use generic event names with agent metadata in payload, update createWorkerAgent/createShadowAgent factories. Old pattern: artist.task.completed. New pattern: task.completed with agentId and agentRole in data field | Restrictions: Must maintain backward compatibility during migration (support both old and new patterns temporarily), ensure kadi-broker topic exchange wildcards still work, do not break existing event subscribers | Success: Events use generic names, agent metadata included in payload, existing subscribers still receive events, multiple workers with same role produce distinguishable events_

- [ ] 12. Update agent-producer event subscribers for generic events
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agent-producer\src\event-handlers\
  - Update all KADI event subscribers in agent-producer to handle generic event names with agent metadata in payload
  - Migrate from role-specific subscriptions (e.g., artist.task.*) to generic subscriptions (e.g., task.*) with payload filtering
  - Purpose: Enable agent-producer to work with generic event naming
  - _Leverage: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agent-producer\src\event-handlers\, C:\GitHub\AGENTS\kadi-core\ (topic exchange pattern matching)_
  - _Requirements: 2.3, 2.4, 5.1_
  - _Prompt: Role: Backend Developer with expertise in event-driven systems | Task: Update all KADI event subscribers in agent-producer to handle generic event names, migrating from role-specific subscriptions to generic subscriptions with payload-based agent identification | Restrictions: Must handle both old and new event formats during migration period, maintain all existing orchestration logic, test with multiple agent-worker instances | Success: agent-producer correctly receives and processes generic events, can distinguish between different agent-workers by agentId/agentRole in payload, orchestration logic works correctly_

- [ ] 13. Create role configuration files for agent-worker
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agent-worker\config\roles\artist.json
  - Create role configuration files for artist, programmer, and designer roles defining capabilities, maxConcurrentTasks, worktreePath, eventTopic, and commitFormat
  - Follow JSON schema from design.md RoleConfig model and use RoleConfigLoader from agents-library
  - Purpose: Define role-specific configurations for generic worker agent
  - _Leverage: design.md (Component 2 RoleConfig model), C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agents-library\src\role-config-loader.ts_
  - _Requirements: 2.1_
  - _Prompt: Role: Configuration Engineer with expertise in JSON schema design | Task: Create role configuration files for artist, programmer, and designer roles following design.md RoleConfig model, defining capabilities, maxConcurrentTasks, worktreePath, eventTopic, and commitFormat for each role | Restrictions: Must follow JSON schema from design.md, ensure all required fields are present, use valid file paths, configs must be loadable by RoleConfigLoader from agents-library | Success: Three role configuration files created, JSON is valid, all required fields present, configurations match design.md schema, RoleConfigLoader loads them successfully_

- [ ] 14. Implement RoleLoader and RoleValidator in agent-worker
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agent-worker\src\roles\RoleLoader.ts
  - Implement RoleLoader and RoleValidator classes that wrap agents-library RoleConfigLoader with agent-worker-specific validation
  - Handle file not found errors and return clear error messages for invalid configurations
  - Purpose: Load and validate role configurations in agent-worker context
  - _Leverage: design.md (Component 2 interfaces), C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agents-library\src\role-config-loader.ts_
  - _Requirements: 2.1, 2.5_
  - _Prompt: Role: Backend Developer with expertise in configuration management | Task: Implement RoleLoader and RoleValidator classes that wrap agents-library RoleConfigLoader with agent-worker-specific validation following design.md Component 2 interfaces | Restrictions: Must reuse RoleConfigLoader from agents-library, handle file not found errors, validate all required fields, return clear error messages for invalid configurations | Success: RoleLoader loads configurations correctly using agents-library, RoleValidator validates all fields, error messages are clear and actionable_

- [ ] 15. Refactor AgentCore to use role configuration
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agent-worker\src\core\AgentCore.ts
  - Refactor AgentCore to use role configuration for initialization, extracting role-specific logic into configuration files
  - Support environment variable AGENT_ROLE or CLI argument --role
  - Purpose: Enable generic worker agent with role-based initialization
  - _Leverage: design.md (Component 2 interfaces), C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agent-artist\src\core\AgentCore.ts_
  - _Requirements: 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4_
  - _Prompt: Role: Backend Developer with expertise in agent architecture | Task: Refactor AgentCore to use role configuration for initialization following design.md Component 2 interfaces, extracting role-specific logic into configuration files and supporting environment variable AGENT_ROLE or CLI argument --role | Restrictions: Must preserve all existing functionality, maintain backward compatibility with agent-artist, handle role initialization errors gracefully, use agents-library factories | Success: AgentCore initializes with role configuration, all three roles work correctly, backward compatibility maintained_

- [ ] 16. Test role-based initialization
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agent-worker\src\index.ts
  - Test role-based initialization for artist, programmer, and designer roles
  - Verify agent starts successfully with correct capabilities and event topics for each role
  - Purpose: Validate role-based initialization works for all roles
  - _Leverage: Role configuration files from task 13_
  - _Requirements: 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4_
  - _Prompt: Role: QA Engineer with expertise in agent testing | Task: Test role-based initialization for artist, programmer, and designer roles, verifying agent starts successfully with correct capabilities and event topics for each role | Restrictions: Must test all three roles, verify environment variable and CLI argument work, test error handling for invalid roles | Success: All three roles initialize correctly, capabilities match configuration, event topics are correct_

- [ ] 17. Test task execution with role-specific capabilities
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agent-worker\src\core\TaskExecutor.ts
  - Test task execution for each role (artist, programmer, designer) verifying tasks are executed according to role capabilities
  - Verify correct tools are invoked for each role
  - Purpose: Validate task execution works with role-specific capabilities
  - _Leverage: design.md (Component 2 interfaces)_
  - _Requirements: 2.6_
  - _Prompt: Role: QA Engineer with expertise in task execution testing | Task: Test task execution for each role (artist, programmer, designer) verifying tasks are executed according to role capabilities and correct tools are invoked | Restrictions: Must test all three roles, verify capabilities are enforced, test error handling for unsupported capabilities | Success: Tasks execute correctly for each role, capabilities are enforced, error messages are clear_

- [ ] 18. Test git commit with role-specific format
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agent-worker\src\core\GitHelper.ts
  - Test git commit creation for each role verifying commit messages follow role-specific format from configuration
  - Purpose: Validate git commits use role-specific format
  - _Leverage: design.md (Component 2 RoleConfig commitFormat)_
  - _Requirements: 2.7_
  - _Prompt: Role: QA Engineer with expertise in git testing | Task: Test git commit creation for each role verifying commit messages follow role-specific format from configuration | Restrictions: Must test all three roles, verify commit format matches configuration | Success: Git commits use correct format for each role, commits are created successfully_

- [ ] 19. Update documentation for agent-worker
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agent-worker\README.md
  - Update README.md and CLAUDE.md for agent-worker documenting role-based configuration system
  - Include role configuration schema, environment variable usage, CLI arguments, and examples for each role
  - Purpose: Document role-based agent configuration
  - _Leverage: structure.md documentation patterns_
  - _Requirements: 2.7_
  - _Prompt: Role: Technical Writer with expertise in developer documentation | Task: Update README.md and CLAUDE.md for agent-worker documenting role-based configuration system, including role configuration schema, environment variable usage, CLI arguments, and examples for each role | Restrictions: Must follow structure.md documentation patterns, include clear examples, document all configuration fields | Success: Documentation is clear and comprehensive, examples work correctly, all configuration fields documented_

- [ ] 20. Verify backward compatibility with agent-artist
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agent-worker\src\index.ts
  - Verify agent-worker with artist role configuration produces identical behavior to agent-artist
  - Test all existing functionality including task execution, git operations, and KADI integration
  - Purpose: Ensure agent-worker can replace agent-artist without breaking changes
  - _Leverage: Existing agent-artist functionality_
  - _Requirements: 2.7_
  - _Prompt: Role: QA Engineer with expertise in backward compatibility testing | Task: Verify agent-worker with artist role configuration produces identical behavior to agent-artist, testing all existing functionality including task execution, git operations, and KADI integration | Restrictions: Must test all existing agent-artist functionality, verify no breaking changes, test with real tasks | Success: agent-worker with artist role behaves identically to agent-artist, all functionality works, no breaking changes_

- [ ] 21. Create shadow role configuration files
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\shadow-agent-worker\config\roles\artist.json
  - Create shadow role configuration files for artist, programmer, and designer roles defining workerWorktreePath, shadowWorktreePath, workerBranch, shadowBranch, and monitoringInterval
  - Follow JSON schema from design.md ShadowRoleConfig model
  - Purpose: Define shadow role-specific configurations
  - _Leverage: design.md (Component 3 ShadowRoleConfig model)_
  - _Requirements: 3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4_
  - _Prompt: Role: Configuration Engineer with expertise in JSON schema design | Task: Create shadow role configuration files for artist, programmer, and designer roles following design.md ShadowRoleConfig model, defining workerWorktreePath, shadowWorktreePath, workerBranch, shadowBranch, and monitoringInterval for each role | Restrictions: Must follow JSON schema from design.md, ensure all required fields are present, use valid file paths | Success: Three shadow role configuration files created, JSON is valid, all required fields present, configurations match design.md schema_

- [ ] 22. Implement ShadowRoleLoader and ShadowRoleValidator
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\shadow-agent-worker\src\roles\ShadowRoleLoader.ts
  - Implement ShadowRoleLoader and ShadowRoleValidator classes that wrap agents-library RoleConfigLoader with shadow-specific validation
  - Handle file not found errors and return clear error messages for invalid configurations
  - Purpose: Load and validate shadow role configurations
  - _Leverage: design.md (Component 3 interfaces), C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agents-library\src\role-config-loader.ts_
  - _Requirements: 3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4_
  - _Prompt: Role: Backend Developer with expertise in configuration management | Task: Implement ShadowRoleLoader and ShadowRoleValidator classes that wrap agents-library RoleConfigLoader with shadow-specific validation following design.md Component 3 interfaces | Restrictions: Must reuse RoleConfigLoader from agents-library, handle file not found errors, validate all required fields | Success: ShadowRoleLoader loads configurations correctly using agents-library, ShadowRoleValidator validates all fields, error messages are clear_

- [ ] 23. Refactor ShadowCore to use shadow role configuration
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\shadow-agent-worker\src\core\ShadowCore.ts
  - Refactor ShadowCore to use shadow role configuration for initialization, extracting role-specific logic into configuration files
  - Support all three roles (artist, programmer, designer)
  - Purpose: Enable generic shadow agent with role-based initialization
  - _Leverage: design.md (Component 3 interfaces), C:\GitHub\AGENTS\shadow-agent-artist\src\core\ShadowCore.ts_
  - _Requirements: 3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4, 3.5, 3.6, 3.7_
  - _Prompt: Role: Backend Developer with expertise in shadow agent architecture | Task: Refactor ShadowCore to use shadow role configuration for initialization following design.md Component 3 interfaces, extracting role-specific logic into configuration files and supporting all three roles | Restrictions: Must preserve all existing functionality, maintain backward compatibility with shadow-agent-artist, handle role initialization errors gracefully, use agents-library factories | Success: ShadowCore initializes with shadow role configuration, all three roles work correctly, backward compatibility maintained_

- [ ] 24. Test monitoring and rollback functionality
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\shadow-agent-worker\src\core\Monitor.ts
  - Test shadow agent monitoring and rollback functionality for all three roles
  - Verify health checks work, anomaly detection works, and rollback restores previous git state correctly
  - Purpose: Validate shadow agent monitoring and rollback works for all roles
  - _Leverage: design.md (Component 3 interfaces)_
  - _Requirements: 3.5, 3.6, 3.7_
  - _Prompt: Role: QA Engineer with expertise in shadow agent testing | Task: Test shadow agent monitoring and rollback functionality for all three roles, verifying health checks work, anomaly detection works, and rollback restores previous git state correctly | Restrictions: Must test all three roles, verify monitoring interval is respected, test rollback with real git operations | Success: Monitoring works for all roles, anomaly detection triggers correctly, rollback restores git state successfully_

- [ ] 25. DaemonAgent generic command system (reference existing spec)
  - File: C:\GitHub\DaemonAgent\.spec-workflow\specs\generic-command-system\
  - Follow the existing generic-command-system spec at C:\GitHub\DaemonAgent\.spec-workflow\specs\generic-command-system\ which contains comprehensive requirements.md (8 requirement categories, 60+ acceptance criteria) and design.md
  - Create tasks.md for the generic-command-system spec and execute tasks within the DaemonAgent repository
  - Purpose: Implement DaemonAgent generic command system as defined by its own dedicated spec
  - _Leverage: C:\GitHub\DaemonAgent\.spec-workflow\specs\generic-command-system\requirements.md, C:\GitHub\DaemonAgent\.spec-workflow\specs\generic-command-system\design.md_
  - _Requirements: 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 4.5, 4.6, 4.7_
  - _Prompt: Role: C++ Developer with expertise in game engine architecture and V8 integration | Task: Follow the existing generic-command-system spec to implement the full generic command system. First create tasks.md for the spec, then execute each task. The spec covers Generic Command Structure, Runtime Handler Registry, JavaScript CommandQueue API, Thread Safety with V8 Integration, Safety Measures, Performance Characteristics, Async Callback Support, and Migration Path from Typed Commands | Restrictions: Must follow the existing spec exactly, do not deviate from requirements.md acceptance criteria, maintain thread safety with V8 integration, follow two-phase migration strategy | Success: All 60+ acceptance criteria from the generic-command-system spec are met, commands register and execute correctly, V8 integration is thread-safe, migration from typed commands is complete_

- [ ] 26. Test quest creation and task assignment workflow
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agent-producer\src\workflows\QuestWorkflow.ts
  - Test complete quest creation and task assignment workflow: agent-producer creates quest, assigns tasks to agent-worker instances, verifies task completion events

  - _Leverage: design.md (Multi-Agent Workflow section)_
  - _Requirements: 5.1, 5.2_
  - _Prompt: Role: Integration Engineer with expertise in multi-agent systems | Task: Test complete quest creation and task assignment workflow with agent-producer creating quests, assigning tasks to agent-worker instances, and verifying task completion events | Restrictions: Must test with multiple agent-worker instances, verify KADI event flow, test with different roles | Success: Quest creation works, task assignment works, completion events received correctly, multiple workers handle tasks concurrently_

- [ ] 27. Test workflow scenarios 1-5 (Parallel, Sequential, Cross-Role, Conflict, Error Recovery)
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agent-producer\src\workflows\
  - Test workflow scenarios 1-5 end-to-end, verifying agent-producer orchestration tools work correctly

  - Purpose: Validate complex multi-agent workflow scenarios
  - _Leverage: design.md (Multi-Agent Workflow section)_
  - _Requirements: 5.1, 5.2, 5.3, 5.4, 5.5, 6.3_

  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\agent-producer\src\workflows\
  - Test workflow scenarios 6-10 end-to-end, verifying all components work together in complex scenarios
  - Purpose: Validate advanced multi-agent workflow scenarios
  - _Leverage: design.md (Multi-Agent Workflow section)_

- [ ] 29. Document workflow scenario test results
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\.spec-workflow\specs\M3-expand-task-complexity\workflow-test-results.md

- [ ] 30. Verify ability-file-management tools
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\mcp-server-quest\src\tools\ability-file-management\
  - Verify all ability-file-management tools work correctly with the new generic agent-worker
  - Test file creation, reading, updating, and deletion through KADI broker

- [ ] 31. Verify ability-quest-management tools
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\mcp-server-quest\src\tools\ability-quest-management\
  - Verify all ability-quest-management tools work correctly with the new generic agent-worker
  - Test quest creation, status updates, task assignment, and completion through KADI broker

- [ ] 32. Verify end-to-end KADI abilities integration
  - File: C:\GitHub\AGENTS\mcp-server-quest\src\tools\
  - Run comprehensive end-to-end test of all KADI abilities across all tool categories
  - Verify cross-tool workflows (e.g., create quest, assign task, execute, complete)

comments.comment: For refactoring agent-artist to agent-worker and shadow-agent-artist to shadow-agent-worker, it might involve C:\GitHub\agnets-library. Please go check if we should put the role related functions in agents-library.

Thinking about there might be multiple agent-worker with the same role name, I think currently, the way agent-artist and shadow-agent-artist publish kadi-event is including the role related info, such as artist.task.completed, etc. Do you think that we should just pub/sub kadi event with generic event name but includes agent specific info, such as agent id? I'm not sure if kadi-core and kadi-broker will support this, please go and check it out for me.

For refacotring DaemonAgent, is it really that simple that these tasks can achieve my goal? I'm asking this because refactoring DaemonAgent was a whole spec. Please go read C:\GitHub\DaemonAgent and C:\GitHub\DaemonEngine and tell me it's current status and I will tell you if it's correct and then we can discuss about what we want to achieve for DaemonAgent.
